dag_desc: Extract the pure graph algebra as a stable protocol standard — language-agnostic, formally verifiable, embeddable by any agent framework. The 400-line core becomes infrastructure other systems build on.

tasks.desc: Init — convergence DAG complete, core/ exists with pure algebra

Define the JSON wire format for Graph, NodeSpec, Position, and ValidationResult. This is the language-agnostic serialization: any implementation in any language that reads and writes this format interoperates. JSON Schema files for each type.

Document the algebraic properties that any implementation must satisfy: define is idempotent, check terminates in O(V+E), orient is monotonic (position never regresses given monotonically growing exists), verify is decidable. These are the proof obligations for alternative implementations.

Property-based tests for the core algebra using fast-check. Generate random valid graphs, verify: define(define(g)) === define(g) (idempotency), orient monotonicity, check termination, verify completeness. These tests validate the algebra without fixtures.

Extend produces/consumes to reference artifacts across repo boundaries: 'peer::<repoId>::<artifact>'. verify() resolves cross-repo consumes via a registry function. This is produces/consumes as a package manager: repos declare what they export and what they need.

Nodes have observable cost (duration, token spend from trail). Agents have observable capability (validator pass rate). Add cost and capability to Position so orient returns not just what to do but what it's worth. The DAG becomes an auction manifest.

The spec → DAG → batch → brief pipeline is a compilation stack. Formalize each stage: SpecIR compiles to Graph (roadmap make), Graph compiles to Batch[] (parallelOrder), Batch compiles to Brief[] (getBrief per node). Each stage has a defined input/output type and is independently testable.

Protocol standard established: JSON wire format, algebraic properties documented and property-tested, cross-repo and economic primitives designed. The algebra is embeddable by any framework in any language.
